title: quercus calliprinos var. recurvans
intro: Quercus calliprinos var. recurvans, also known as the Cypriot oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to Cyprus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its high content of flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. In traditional herbal medicine, Quercus calliprinos var. recurvans is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of traditional teas and tonics.
